players.name: Melvin Mastil
players.bio: A Swiss-based shot-stopper who earned his first cap in 2025, Mastil is the wildcard of this squad. Playing for Stade Nyonnais, he's Algeria's least-capped goalkeeper at this tournament — but everyone starts somewhere.
players.facts: His sole cap makes him one of the least-experienced goalkeepers at the 2026 World Cup. No pressure, Melvin.

players.name: Aïssa Mandi
players.bio: Algeria's defensive rock with 117 caps, Mandi has been at the heart of Les Fennecs' backline for over a decade. The Lille veteran brings Champions League pedigree and an unflappable presence to his second World Cup campaign.
players.facts: One of only a handful of Algerians to surpass 100 caps — a true centurion of the desert fox era.

players.name: Achref Abada
players.bio: Homegrown and proud, Abada is one of the few players in this squad who came through Algerian domestic football with USM Alger. Still building his international résumé at 26, he's a composed centre-back with an eye for goal.
players.facts: Born in Touggourt, a Saharan oasis city — he's used to hot conditions. Perfect for a North African summer.

players.name: Mohamed Amine Tougai
players.bio: A product of Algerian football who found success in Tunis, Tougai has established himself as a reliable centre-back with 29 caps at just 26. Commanding in the air and disciplined on the ground, he's a modern defensive asset.
players.facts: He plays club football in Tunisia for Espérance de Tunis — one of Africa's most decorated club sides.

players.name: Zineddine Belaïd
players.bio: A fiercely local pick, Belaïd plies his trade with JS Kabylie, one of Algeria's most storied clubs. Still earning his international stripes at 27, this World Cup represents a massive stage for the Algerian domestic defender.
players.facts: JS Kabylie have won the CAF Cup twice — Belaïd plays for one of Africa's true cult clubs.

players.name: Ramiz Zerrouki
players.full_name: Ramiz Larbi Zerrouki; رامز العربي زروقي
players.bio: Born in Amsterdam and raised on Dutch football, Zerrouki chose Algeria over the Oranje and hasn't looked back. An engine in Twente's midfield, he's a tireless ball-winner who keeps the gears turning for Les Fennecs.
players.facts: He grew up in Amsterdam — the city of Cruyff. He chose the Fennec Fox over the Clockwork Orange.

players.name: Riyad Mahrez
players.bio: A living legend. Mahrez dazzled Premier League fans at Leicester and Man City before heading to Saudi Arabia, but he remains Algeria's crown jewel at 35. The 2019 AFCON winner is the heartbeat of this squad.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Superfly VII Elite FG
players.facts: Mahrez won the Premier League FIVE times — with two different clubs. Not bad for a lad from Sarcelles.

players.name: Houssem Aouar
players.full_name: Houssem-Eddine Chaâbane Aouar; حُسَام الدِّين شَعْبَان عَوَار
players.bio: Lyon's former wonderkid finally committed to Algeria after courting French interest. Aouar's creativity and vision make him a natural No.10, and his Saudi Pro League spell has added goals to his game. A silky operator.
players.facts: Aouar was heavily linked with Arsenal before joining Roma — he's had more transfer sagas than caps.

players.name: Amine Gouiri
players.full_name: Amine Ferid Gouiri; أمين فريد غويري
players.bio: Gouiri has been one of Ligue 1's most exciting forwards, now starring for Marseille. Quick, technical, and clinical, the 26-year-old chose Algeria over France and has repaid the faith with 8 international goals.
players.facts: He was born in Bourgoin-Jallieu — a town best known for rugby. Football's gain, rugby's loss.

players.name: Farès Chaïbi
players.bio: The youngest of Algeria's Lyonnais exports, Chaïbi has taken the Bundesliga by storm at Eintracht Frankfurt. Creative, direct, and fearless, the 23-year-old wears the No.10 shirt like he was born for it.
players.facts: Born just after the 2002 World Cup — he literally grew up in the tournament era he's now playing in.

players.name: Anis Hadj Moussa
players.bio: A speedy winger who shines in Rotterdam's famous attacking system at Feyenoord, Hadj Moussa is one of Algeria's brightest young sparks. Only 24, he brings pace and directness on the right flank.
players.facts: Feyenoord's Eredivisie champions have produced some of football's greats — Hadj Moussa is next in line.

players.name: Nadhir Benbouali
players.full_name: Ahmed Nadhir Benbouali; أحمد نذير بن بوعلي
players.bio: An unlikely World Cup traveller, Benbouali plays in the Hungarian top flight for Győri ETO but has already found the net internationally. Born in Chlef, he's a testament to Algerian football's wide casting net.
players.facts: He plays in Hungary — making him arguably the most geographically surprising player in this entire squad.

players.name: Jaouen Hadjam
players.bio: One of Swiss football's most dynamic full-backs, Hadjam marauds forward from left-back for Young Boys and has chipped in with 3 goals in 17 caps. At just 23, he's a key future asset for Les Fennecs.
players.facts: Born in Paris in 2003 — he was one year old when Zidane headbutted Materazzi. A different era entirely.

players.name: Hicham Boudaoui
players.bio: A combative and energetic central midfielder who has been a fixture at Nice since his teens, Boudaoui brings grit and intelligence to Algeria's engine room. Born in Béchar, he represents Algerian domestic pride.
players.facts: Boudaoui has made over 150 appearances for Nice — loyalty in an era of constant transfers is genuinely rare.

players.name: Rayan Aït-Nouri
players.bio: From Wolves to Manchester City — Aït-Nouri's rise has been meteoric. Algeria's first-choice left-back is one of the best in that position in the Premier League: attacking, athletic, and almost never troubled defensively.
players.facts: He joined Man City — meaning he now trains at a facility that costs more than most national football budgets.

players.name: Oussama Benbot
players.bio: A veteran of the Algerian domestic scene with USM Alger, Benbot is the experienced backup between the sticks. At 31 with only 2 caps, this World Cup squad call-up is the pinnacle of his international career.
players.facts: Born in Ain M'lila — a city more famous for its steel industry than its goalkeepers. He's changing that.

players.name: Rafik Belghali
players.bio: Born in Leuven, Belgium, Belghali has Italian football experience with Hellas Verona and brings youthful energy to Algeria's right flank. At 24, he's racking up caps and has already scored at international level.
players.facts: He was born in Belgium, plays in Italy, and represents Algeria — a true citizen of world football.

players.name: Mohamed Amoura
players.full_name: Mohamed El Amine Amoura; محمد الأمين عمورة
players.bio: Amoura is Algeria's most lethal current striker — 19 goals in 45 caps is an outstanding return. The VfL Wolfsburg forward is explosive, hard-working, and clinical. A constant threat who loves the big occasion.
players.facts: He was born in Tahir, a small Algerian coastal town. He's now the big fish in the Bundesliga pond.

players.name: Nabil Bentaleb
players.bio: A Lille local who made his name in the Premier League with Spurs, Bentaleb has had a career of peaks and valleys. Now back at Lille, the experienced defensive midfielder brings composure and leadership to Algeria's core.
players.facts: Bentaleb made his Spurs debut as a teenager under Tim Sherwood — a sentence that dates him perfectly.

players.name: Adil Boulbina
players.bio: One of the most prolific young attackers in this squad, Boulbina has a staggering 6 goals in just 11 caps. The 23-year-old left winger in Qatar's league is a goal-per-game threat who could be Algeria's wildcard weapon.
players.facts: 6 goals in 11 caps is a remarkable ratio — only Mahrez among current starters can better it overall.

players.name: Ramy Bensebaini
players.full_name: Amir Selmane Rami Bensebaini; أمير رامي بن سبعيني‎
players.bio: Bensebaini is one of the most dangerous left-backs in world football — a set-piece specialist and tireless runner who thrives at Borussia Dortmund. With 81 caps and 7 goals, he is a cornerstone of this Algeria side.
players.facts: He once scored twice against Mönchengladbach in the Bundesliga — as a defender. Defenders should not do that.

players.name: Ibrahim Maza
players.bio: The jewel of this squad. Born in Berlin and developed at Bayer Leverkusen — the Bundesliga champions — Maza is just 20 but already a full international. A technically gifted playmaker with a huge future ahead.
players.facts: At 20, he's the youngest player in the squad and plays for reigning Bundesliga champions Bayer Leverkusen. No biggie.

players.name: Luca Zidane
players.full_name: Luca Zinedine Zidane Fernández; لوكا زين الدين زيدان
players.bio: Yes, that Zidane — Zinedine's son. Luca has carved out his own path in Spain with Granada, earning 7 caps for Algeria. He's determined to step out of the family shadow and write his own World Cup story.
players.facts: His dad won the 1998 World Cup with France. Luca is at the 2026 World Cup with Algeria. Family tradition upheld.

players.name: Yacine Titraoui
players.full_name: Yacine Mohamed El Ghazali Titraoui; ياسين محمد الغزالي تيطراوي
players.bio: Titraoui is one of the freshest faces in this squad — a 22-year-old central midfielder from M'Sila now playing Belgian football at Charleroi. Only 5 caps to his name, but he's here and ready to make his mark.
players.facts: M'Sila is known as the 'city of roses' in Algeria. Titraoui has bloomed rather quickly himself.

players.name: Farès Ghedjemis
players.bio: Ghedjemis has the most impressive debut stat in the squad — he scored on his only cap. The 23-year-old Frosinone winger is lightning-quick and direct, a player who makes things happen every time he touches the ball.
players.facts: 100% World Cup squad goal-conversion rate on debut cap. Statistically, Algeria's most efficient attacker.

players.name: Samir Chergui
players.full_name: Samir Sophian Chergui; سمير سفيان شرقي
players.bio: A solid centre-back who works hard for Paris FC in France's second tier, Chergui has 4 caps and brings physical presence to Algeria's defensive depth. Born in Arpajon, this World Cup spot is his biggest call-up yet.
players.facts: Playing in Ligue 2 and called to a World Cup squad — proof that hard work and the right coach can change everything.
